prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's services page.

HOT LEAD signals:
- All services dumped on one page with no structure
- No individual service pages (bad for SEO)
- Missing service descriptions — just a bullet list
- No distinction between emergency and scheduled work
- No photos of actual work

COLD LEAD signals:
- Individual pages per service type (residential, commercial, auto, emergency)
- Detailed descriptions of what each service involves
- Clear emergency vs scheduled distinction
- Service-specific CTAs
- Before/after photos
- FAQ per service

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of services presentation>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = worse services page = better lead>",
  "serviceStructure": "<individual-pages|single-page|minimal|none>",
  "servicesListed": ["<service types found>"],
  "hasEmergencyDistinction": true/false,
  "hasServiceDescriptions": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response: {"records":[{"url":"https://www.london-locksmiths.com/london-locksmiths-services.htm","pageName":"London Locksmiths Services","summary":"The services page lists a broad range of locksmith and security offerings across two sections, with each service linking to its own dedicated sub-page. Services are organised into loose groupings (emergency, domestic/commercial, physical security, alarms/CCTV) with brief paragraph-level descriptions rather than per-service detail.","opportunityScore":5,"serviceStructure":"individual-pages","servicesListed":["24 Hour Emergency Locksmiths","Burglary Repairs","Emergency Lock Repair and Replacement","Mobile Locksmiths","Domestic Locksmiths","Commercial Locksmiths","Insurance Surveys","Lock Fitting","UPVC/Patio Door and Lock Repairs","Window Locks","Safe Fitting Opening and Servicing","Door Repair and Carpentry","Access Control","Bars Grills and Shutters","Security Keys","Master Keys and Keyed Alike","Domestic Burglar Alarms","CCTV","Commercial Alarm Systems","Free Security Audits","Free Security Surveys","High Security Locks","Industrial Safe Installation"],"hasEmergencyDistinction":true,"hasServiceDescriptions":false,"topOpportunity":"Individual service pages exist but lack detailed descriptions, FAQs, before/after photos and service-specific CTAs — a content and conversion optimisation project per service page.","notes":"The site has the structural bones of a good services section but each linked page likely lacks depth, trust signals and conversion elements, making it a moderate opportunity for a content-led redesign pitch."},{"url":"https://www.london-locksmiths.com/north-london-locksmiths/index.htm","pageName":"North London Locksmiths","summary":"A geographic coverage page for North London that presents service capabilities as short bullet-point lists (emergency, response times, pricing) with no descriptive content per service type.
